subtasks.description: Extract licensed user counts and SKU details from SAM database, grouped by product/license type and business unit
Query consumption facts to retrieve active user counts, spend, and usage metrics by product/SKU and business unit

Reconcile SAM licensed users against consumption facts active users; calculate provisioning variance (over/under by %, by unit); classify each product/BU as right-sized, over-provisioned, or under-provisioned; identify cost optimization opportunities

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks needed: (1) SAM licensing extraction is a single mongo_query (small tier) to pull licensed user inventories; (2) consumption facts is a single sql_gen (mid tier) requiring multi-table joins across usage and spend tables to align metrics; (3) cross_recon (frontier tier) handles the reconciliation logic, variance calculation, posture classification (over/under-provisioning), and optimization recommendation generation across multiple products/BUs. Frontier tier required for cross_recon due to multi-signal variance analysis, classification heuristics, and business unit segmentation. This is NOT a policy_action since no governance approval or compliance check is requested—it is a posture analysis and cost optimization recommendation task native to reconciliation.

output.sql: SELECT product, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS active_users, SUM(amount_usd) AS total_spend, AVG(daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_active_seats, MAX(daily_active_seats) AS max_daily_active_seats, MIN(daily_active_seats) AS min_daily_active_seats, COUNT(DISTINCT license_id) AS licensed_count, SUM(daily_active_seats) AS total_seat_days, COUNT(DISTINCT event_id) AS total_events, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'usage' THEN amount_usd ELSE 0 END) AS usage_spend, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'license' THEN amount_usd ELSE 0 END) AS license_spend, ROUND(AVG(amount_usd), 2) AS avg_spend_per_event, ROUND(SUM(amount_usd) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT license_id), 0), 2) AS spend_per_license, ROUND(SUM(amount_usd) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT user_id), 0), 2) AS spend_per_active_user, (COUNT(DISTINCT license_id) - COUNT(DISTINCT user_id)) AS provisioning_gap, CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT license_id) > COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) THEN 'Over-Provisioned' WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT license_id) < COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) THEN 'Under-Provisioned' ELSE 'Balanced' END AS provisioning_status FROM consumption_facts WHERE publisher ILIKE '%Microsoft%' GROUP BY product ORDER BY provisioning_gap DESC, total_spend DESC
